This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Latin America, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon ekmanii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Koanophyllon ekmanii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
